name: Frogger
description: Frogger is a single-player arcade game where the player controls a frog who must navigate a series of increasingly dangerous obstacles to reach home. The playing field is divided into two main sections: the road and the river. The road section features multiple lanes of traffic moving horizontally at varying speeds and directions. The river section features logs and turtles also moving horizontally, which the frog can ride on to avoid falling into the water. At the top of the screen are five 'home' slots which the player needs to fill with frogs to complete the level. The frog starts at the bottom of the screen and can only move in the four cardinal directions (up, down, left, right) in discrete jumps. The player must guide the frog across the road, dodging oncoming cars, and then across the river, hopping onto moving logs and turtles. Landing in the water or being hit by a vehicle results in the loss of a life. Certain logs and turtles submerge for brief periods, presenting additional hazards. Completing a level (filling all five home slots) advances the player to the next, with increasing difficulty (faster traffic, shorter log/turtle segments, more frequent submerging, etc.). Bonus points are awarded for completing a level quickly, and for collecting insects that may appear in the home slots. Some home slots might also contain alligators who can eat the frog. The game ends when the player runs out of lives.
